metadata.purpose: Data Dictionary: every clinical variable per section with specific values, thresholds, and types as they appear in recommendation text. Used by CMI for patient scenario matching and by the deterministic section router for concept intersection.
v1_known_issues: 4.4 and 4.5 have duplicated content in guideline_knowledge.json — glucose values leak into 4.4, temperature values leak into 4.5
4.6.2 has 0 recs in guideline_knowledge.json but has criteria in recommendation_criteria.json
Values listed are those explicitly stated in recommendation text, not inferred
v2_changes: Normalized type taxonomy to v2 canonical types: ['categorical', 'dose', 'mixed_needs_review', 'modality_enum', 'process_metric', 'qualifier_enum', 'range', 'reperfusion_grade_enum', 'structured_doses', 'structured_thresholds', 'systolic_bp_threshold', 'threshold', 'time_window_hours', 'volume_range_mL']
Added source_rec_ids[] and synonym_term_ids[] to every variable (empty; for human reviewer to populate)
Added parsed_values for BP, SpO2, time_window, glucose, drug_dose, age, NIHSS variables — structured numeric representation for Python arithmetic
Linked intervention[] values to synonym_dictionary term IDs where a high-confidence mapping exists; unlinked values flagged in unlinked_values[]

Added review_flags[] at section level for sections with suspected cross-section leakage (4.1, 4.2) and known v1 data-quality issues (4.4, 4.5, 4.6.2). Original values PRESERVED — values are flagged for review, not deleted.
changes_needing_human_review: Cross-section leakage verification for sections flagged in review_flags (see individual sections)
Fill source_rec_ids[] on every variable (currently empty) by tracing back to guideline_knowledge.json
Verify parsed_values block for all threshold/range/dose variables against the original strings
usage_notes: Every variable now has source_rec_ids[] and synonym_term_ids[]; populate these during review
parsed_values is present for numeric variables — Python should prefer parsed_values over values for arithmetic comparison
review_flags at section level indicates sections with known or suspected data quality issues; route-around logic should treat these sections with lower confidence until resolved

Ambulance Transport.note: Prehospital assessment and treatment during transport

No local access to TSC.note: When TSC is not locally available

Telestroke in SSOC.note: Telestroke within stroke systems of care

SpO2.note: 93% lower target, 100% upper limit — do not use supplemental O2 if SpO2 >93%

review_flags.concern: Section is about Airway/Breathing/Oxygenation. v1 has vessel=[LVO] and time_window spanning 2h-72h which look like values leaked from EVT/IVT recommendations that happen to co-occur in the corpus. SpO2 thresholds (93%, 100%) are legitimate.
Section is Head Positioning. v1 has vessel=[LVO, MCA], NIHSS=4, premorbid_mRS=0, perfusion imaging — all look like leakage from adjacent recommendations. Only head-of-bed-angle values should be here.

General (without reperfusion).note: BP thresholds when no IVT/EVT planned

Before reperfusion treatment.note: Pre-IVT and pre-EVT BP eligibility criteria

After EVT.note: Post-thrombectomy BP targets including TICI-based guidance

4.4.title: Temperature Management
4.4.data_quality_note: JSON has duplicated content with section 4.5

review_flags.concern: v1 metadata flags duplicated content with 4.5 (glucose values leaked in). Current v1 temperature value 37.5C is legit; verify no glucose values present.

4.5.title: Blood Glucose Management
4.5.data_quality_note: JSON has duplicated content with section 4.4

review_flags.concern: v1 metadata flags duplicated content with 4.4 (temperature values leaked in). Current v1 glucose structured_thresholds are legit; verify no temperature values present.

values.alteplase: 0.9 mg/kg (max 90 mg), 10% bolus then 60 min infusion

alteplase.original: 0.9 mg/kg (max 90 mg), 10% bolus then 60 min infusion

General principles.note: Core IVT eligibility and decision criteria

NIHSS.note: NIHSS 0-5 = mild stroke; disabling vs non-disabling is key distinction

ASPECTS.note: Very low ASPECTS = large established infarct

glucose.note: 50 = must treat hypoglycemia first; glucose mimics stroke

categorical.note: Disabling vs non-disabling is a critical criterion for mild stroke IVT eligibility

4.6.2.title: Choice of Thrombolytic Agent
4.6.2.data_quality_note: 0 recs in guideline_knowledge.json — criteria exist in recommendation_criteria.json

review_flags.concern: v1 metadata flags: 0 recs in guideline_knowledge.json but criteria exist in recommendation_criteria.json. Section content is incomplete.

General.note: Extended window IVT criteria — all recs cover imaging-based selection

6-24h, ASPECTS 3-5.note: Large core EVT in extended window

0-6h, mild preexisting disability.note: EVT with premorbid mRS indicating mild disability

0-6h, moderate preexisting disability.note: EVT with premorbid mRS indicating moderate disability

0-6h, dominant proximal M2.note: EVT for dominant hemisphere proximal M2 occlusion

0-6h, nondominant M2/distal/PCA.note: EVT for nondominant M2, distal, or PCA occlusions

ASPECTS.note: 6+ = favorable; 3-5 = large core eligible in some windows; 0-2 = generally excluded

age.note: Age >80 excluded from large core EVT (ASPECTS 3-5) in 6-24h window

premorbid_mRS.note: mRS 0-1 standard; mRS 2 = COR 2a; mRS 3-4 = COR 2b

Thrombectomy general.note: Stent retrievers, aspiration, TICI grading, anesthesia, balloon-guided catheters

Adjunctive techniques.note: MeVO, tandem occlusions, rescue IA thrombolytics, tirofiban

DAPT for minor AIS.note: Dual antiplatelet for minor stroke without high-risk TIA qualifier

NIHSS.note: NIHSS <=3 or <=5 for minor stroke DAPT eligibility

5.1.title: Stroke Units
5.1.note: No specific clinical variable thresholds in rec text

5.5.title: Depression
5.5.note: No specific clinical variable thresholds in rec text

age.note: <=60 = COR 1, LOE A; >60 = COR 2b, LOE B-R
